tex: Write 1.8972 correct to 2 decimal places.
Factorise $2x^2 - x$.

Giulio's reaction times are measured in two games.
In the first game his reaction time is $\dfrac{1}{3}$ of a second.
In the second game his reaction time is $\dfrac{1}{8}$ of a second.
Find the difference between the two reaction times.

The table shows the different methods of travel for 20 people going to work.
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|}
\hline
Method of travel & Frequency \\
\hline
Car & 10 \\
Walk & 5 \\
Bike & 3 \\
Bus & 2 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
Which type of average, mean, median or mode, can be used for this information?

Rearrange $2(w + h) = P$ to make $w$ the subject.

$18m^3 + 9m^2 + 14m + 7 = (9m^2 + 7)$ ()

One solution of the equation $ax^2 + a = 150$ is $x = 7$.

(a) Find the value of $a$.

(b) Find the other solution.

A is the point $(7, 12)$ and B is the point $(2, -1)$.
Find the length of AB.

$A = \dfrac{b \times h}{2}$
$A = 10$, correct to the nearest whole number.
$h = 4$, correct to the nearest whole number.
Work out the upper bound for the value of $b$.
Simplify $\dfrac{x^3 + 5x^2}{x^2 - 25}$, giving your answer as a single fraction.

$y$ is inversely proportional to the square of $(x + 1)$.
$y = 0.875$ when $x = 1$.
Find $y$ when $x = 4$.

J, K, L and M are points on the circumference of a circle with diameter JL.
JL and KM intersect at N.
Angle JNK = 104° and angle MLJ = 22°.
Work out the value of $d$.

Find
(a) $5A$,

(b) $A + B$,

(c) $AB$.

AB = s and AD = t.

160 students record the amount of time, $t$ hours, they each spend playing computer games in a week.
This information is shown in the cumulative frequency diagram.

%\begin{InsertScreenShot}
%    
%\end{InsertScreenShot}

(a) Use the diagram to find an estimate of

(i) (b) the median,

(c) the interquartile range.

(d) Use the diagram to complete this frequency table.

\begin{tabular}{|l|l|}
\hline
Time ($t$ hours) & Frequency \\
\hline
$0 \leq t < 2$ & 20 \\
$2 \leq t < 4$ &  \\
$4 \leq t < 6$ &  \\
$6 \leq t < 8$ &  \\
$8 \leq t < 10$ & 24 \\
$10 \leq t < 12$ & 12 \\
$12 \leq t < 14$ & 4 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
